Drunk again, Deb goes to the station and tells Quinn she killed LaGuerta. Meanwhile, Dexter and the police check out Lyle ...

episodes.name: Scar Tissue
episodes.summary: The next potential killer on Vogel's list is A.J. Yates, who was institutionalized at 15. Vogel tried to channel his hostilities much as she did with Dexter but was unable to do so. When Dexter sees a huge scar on his head, he learns from Vogel that Yates had surgery to remove a brain lesion. He breaks into Yates' house and finds something interesting in the closet. Dexter isn't alone in the house however and it looks he may be their man. He also finds something that affects his relationship with Vogel. Dexter meets a neighbor, Cassie, from the next door apartment. ...

episodes.name: This Little Piggy
episodes.summary: Dr. Vogel tells Dexter that Deb was traumatized when she tried to kill him. Now that she's hit rock bottom, things will improve. Dexter isn't too happy with either Vogel or Deb and concentrates on finding serial killer A.J. Yates. Deb and Dexter have another problem when Yates kidnaps Vogel and they set out to rescue her. At the station, the police continue investigating the death of Norma Rivera. She had sex not long before dying and they assume that whoever that was is also the man who killed her. They have a suspect, her former employer Ed Hamilton, who admits to ...

episodes.name: A Little Reflection
episodes.summary: Dexter is convinced that Zack Hamilton killed Norma Rivera but has no proof and takes to following him whenever he can. He's more than a bit surprised to follow him to Dr. Vogel's office. She admits that Zach is a new patient but refuses to talk about his case. Batista is under pressure from Deputy Chief Tom Matthews to make a decision on who to promote to sergeant. When Quinn learns he didn't get the job, he redoubles his effort to solve the Rivera murder. With Dexter tagging along, it becomes obvious - to Dexter at least - that Zack is stalking his next victim, ...

episodes.name: Dress Code
episodes.summary: After being drugged by Hannah McKay, Dexter wakes up in an industrial area outside of town. He knows that Hannah is responsible and she's surprisingly easy to find, which makes Dexter more than just a bit suspicious. It turns out she's living on a luxury yacht with her husband Jacob. She's changed her name to Maggie and says she's quite happy. Her husband doesn't appreciate the fact that she's met with Dexter forcing her to take action and for Dexter to help her out. Meanwhile, Dexter has agreed to teach Zach Hamilton the code - to Dr. Vogel's delight - but he is ...

episodes.name: Are We There Yet?
episodes.summary: Dexter is certain that Zach Hamilton killed his next door neighbor Cassie, who is found bludgeoned to death in her apartment. The scene suggests that the crime was impulsive rather than planned, as was the case with the death of Norma Rivera. Dexter finds Zach's blood under her fingernails but can't report it for obvious reasons. Zach is in the Keys - he's used his credit card - and Dexter and Hannah set off to find him. What he finds there impresses Dexter but also suggests that Zach isn't the killer. Meanwhile, Dexter is also helping Hannah acquire a new identity ...

episodes.name: Make Your Own Kind of Music
episodes.summary: Dexter has decided to make a new life for himself with Hannah and Harrison but not before he takes care of the Brain Surgeon. In searching Zach Hamilton's photo studio he finds a clump of hair and blood hidden under a table. When Dexter runs the sample against the DNA database he finds a familial match - to Dr. Evelyn Vogel. Somewhat incredulous at his revelation, Vogel explains that she once had two sons, the eldest of which killed the other. He was placed in a mental institution but died in fire. Dexter believes he must have survived and soon has him identified. Deb...

episodes.name: Goodbye Miami
episodes.summary: Having made the decision that he and Harrison will move to Argentina with Hannah, Dexter has a lot to do. He tells Deb about his plan but that doesn't go over very well. He also tells Dr, Vogel that her son, Saxon, is dangerous and she is in need of protection. She insists that he back off and let her deal with him in her own way but Dexter has no intention of leaving Miami while Saxon is alive. Saxon has problems however and he takes his mother to his killing room explaining that it's identical to the treatment room at the mental hospital she put him in. He wants his...

episodes.name: Monkey in a Box
episodes.summary: Still coming to terms with Dr. Vogel's death, Dexter wants to eliminate Oliver Saxon before he, Harrison and Hannah head off to South America. He also has a lot of personal items to tie up including selling his condo. Saxon shows up as a prospective buyer and offers Dexter a deal: they stop going after each other and go their own way. Dexter is non-committal and, having located his kill room, sets a trap for him. Meanwhile, U.S. Deputy Marshall Clayton is following Deb, certain that she will lead him to Hannah. Dexter is continuing to reevaluate his priorities and his...

episodes.name: Remember the Monsters?
episodes.summary: Dexter has to act quickly when Hannah tells him Elway is staking out the airport. He finds a way to get him out of the way but it also leads to the entire airport being closed. With a hurricane bearing down on Miami, few options are open to them. Dexter races to the hospital when he learns that Deb has been shot. She seems to be recovering and he decides to send Hannah and Harrison to Jacksonville where they could catch a flight. A return trip to the hospital leads to Saxon's arrest but also bad news about Deb. Realizing that he has, directly or indirectly, been ...

shows.name: The Office
shows.summary: Steve Carell stars in The Office, a fresh and funny mockumentary-style glimpse into the daily interactions of the eccentric workers at the Dunder Mifflin paper supply company. Based on the smash-hit British series of the same name and adapted for American Television by Greg Daniels, this fast-paced comedy parodies contemporary American water-cooler culture. Earnest but clueless regional manager Michael Scott believes himself to be an exceptional boss and mentor, but actually receives more eye-rolls than respect from his oddball staff.

episodes.name: Pilot
episodes.summary: A documentary crew arrives at Dundler Mifflin to observe the workplace. Michael Scott tries to paint a happy picture while facing potential downsizing.

episodes.name: Diversity Day
episodes.summary: Corporate sends in a consultant after Michael attempts to imitate a Chris Rock routine. Michael ends up staging his own Diversity Day event.

episodes.name: Health Care
episodes.summary: Dwight ends up in charge of picking a new health care plan.

episodes.name: The Alliance
episodes.summary: With Dwight worried about downsizing, Jim agrees to an alliance; Michael tries to increase moral be throwing a birthday party for Meredith.

episodes.name: Basketball
episodes.summary: Michael challenges the warehouse staff to a basketball game versus the office staff.

episodes.name: Hot Girl
episodes.summary: Several male staff members vie for the attention of an attractive purse sales woman.

episodes.name: The Dundies
episodes.summary: Michael hosts the annual Dundies awards.

episodes.name: Sexual Harassment
episodes.summary: Michael's continual sending of explicit e-mail forwards forces Dunder Mifflin to review it's sexual harassment policy; Pam's mom makes a visit to the office.

episodes.name: Office Olympics
episodes.summary: With Michael and Dwight out of the house, Jim organizes an office Olympics.

episodes.name: The Fire
episodes.summary: A fire starts in the office kitchen.

episodes.name: Halloween
episodes.summary: Michael has to fire someone on Halloween.

episodes.name: The Fight
episodes.summary: Michael challenges Dwight to a fight at his martial arts studio.

episodes.name: The Client
episodes.summary: Jan helps Michael with an important pitch for a client; The staff discovers a screenplay in Michael's office.

episodes.name: Performance Review
episodes.summary: Michael uses each employee's performance review to discuss his relationship with Jan.

episodes.name: E-Mail Surveillance
episodes.summary: The office staff finds out that Michael has set up e-mail surveillance.

episodes.name: Christmas Party
episodes.summary: At the office Christmas party, Michael changes secret santa into a gift exchange.

episodes.name: Booze Cruise
episodes.summary: Michael takes the staff on a booze cruise.

episodes.name: The Injury
episodes.summary: Michael accidentally burns his foot on his George Foreman Grill, but insists on coming to work anyway. When none of his employees, except for Dwight , help him, Michael feels under-appreciated. Meanwhile, Dwight starts acting strangely nice to everyone, especially Pam.

episodes.name: The Secret
episodes.summary: Jim is forced into spending time with Michael so that he will not reveal Jim's feelings for Pam. Meanwhile, Oscar takes a "sick day" and Dwight investigates whether he is actually sick.

episodes.name: The Carpet
episodes.summary: A disgusting "thing" – implied to be human feces – is left in Michael's office, and he tries to discover who did it. While his carpet is being replaced, he uses Jim's desk, forcing him to move away from Pam into the annex.

episodes.name: Boys and Girls
episodes.summary: When Jan comes to the office for a women in the office session, Michael stages a men's only session.

episodes.name: Valentine's Day
episodes.summary: Michael has a big meeting in New York with Jan and the companies new CEO; Pam deals with all the Valentine's Day deliveries hoping Roy will remember to send her something.

episodes.name: Dwight's Speech
episodes.summary: Dwight has to make a big speech when he is named Salesmen of the Year.

episodes.name: Take Your Daughter to Work Day
episodes.summary: Staff members bring their children to work.

episodes.name: Michael's Birthday
episodes.summary: Michael's birthday coincides with Kevin awaiting the news to see if he has skin cancer.

episodes.name: Drug Testing
episodes.summary: After Dwight finds a joint in the office park, he goes overboard with an investigation.

episodes.name: Conflict Resolution
episodes.summary: Michael's attempt to resolve conflict resolution in the office just leads to more conflict.

episodes.name: Casino Night
episodes.summary: The office holds a casino night for charity in the warehouse.

episodes.name: Gay Witch Hunt
episodes.summary: Michael finds out that Oscar is gay after Oscar files a complaint when Michael uses a homosexual slur.

episodes.name: The Convention
episodes.summary: Michael and Dwight go to a convention that Jim is also attending; Pam goes on her first date after her engagement.

episodes.name: The Coup
episodes.summary: After Jan discovers Michael has been having "movie Monday's", Angela encourages Dwight to make a move for Michael's job.

episodes.name: Grief Counseling
episodes.summary: Michael organizes grief counseling sessions following the death of his old boss, Ed Truck.

episodes.name: Initiation
episodes.summary: Dwight initiates Ryan into his new job as a salesmen at Dunder Mifflin; Pam is tasked with keeping track of everything Michael does during the day by Jan.

episodes.name: Diwali
episodes.summary: Kelly invites the office staff to a Diwali celebration.

episodes.name: Branch Closing
episodes.summary: Jan gives Michael the news that the Scranton branch is closing.

episodes.name: The Merger
episodes.summary: Jim and other members of the Stamford branch have their first day at Scranton.

episodes.name: The Convict
episodes.summary: The office discovers than one of the new Stamford people is a former convict.

episodes.name: A Benihana Christmas (1)
episodes.summary: Michael gets dumped by Carol; Pam and Karen start a rival party planning committee.

episodes.name: A Benihana Christmas (2)
episodes.summary: Michael gets dumped by Carol; Karen and Pam start a rival party planning committee.

episodes.name: Back from Vacation
episodes.summary: Michael returns from his vacation and accidentally sends a picture of Jan sunbathing to everyone in the office.

episodes.name: Traveling Salesmen
episodes.summary: Dwight does a favor for Angela that puts him in hot water with Michael; The sales team heads out on partner sales calls.

episodes.name: The Return
episodes.summary: Oscar returns to work; Dwight gets a job at Staples.

episodes.name: Ben Franklin
episodes.summary: The ladies of the office throw a bridal shower for Phyllis; Michael tries to throw a rival bachelor party for Bob.

episodes.name: Phyllis' Wedding
episodes.summary: Michael tries to expand his role at Phyllis' wedding; Pam realizes that Phyllis stole all of her ideas from her canceled wedding to Roy.

episodes.name: Business School
episodes.summary: Michael is a guest speaker at Ryan's business school class; A bat gets lose in the office; Pam has an art show that she invites the entire office too.

episodes.name: Cocktails
episodes.summary: David Wallace hosts a party at his host which Michael, Jan, Dwight, Jim and Karen attend; The rest of the office staff go out for Happy Hour.

episodes.name: The Negotiation
episodes.summary: Dwight saves Jim when Roy shows up at the office planning to hit him for kissing Pam; Darryl asks Michael for a raise which leads to Michael asking for a raise from Jan.

episodes.name: Safety Training
episodes.summary: The office staff undergoes safety training; Andy returns from anger management training.

episodes.name: Product Recall
episodes.summary: The office staff deal with a breach in quality control when an obscene water mark ends up their paper.

episodes.name: Women's Appreciation
episodes.summary: After Phyllis is flashed in the parking lot, Michael arranges a women's appreciation day.

episodes.name: Beach Games
episodes.summary: The office attends beach day which Michael uses to try and determine who would replace him if he gets a job at corporate.

episodes.name: The Job (1)
episodes.summary: Michael, Jim and Karen interview for a job at corporate; Michael appoints Dwight as regional manager.

episodes.name: The Job (2)
episodes.summary: Michael, Jim and Karen interview for a job at corporate; Michael appoints Dwight as regional manager.

episodes.name: Fun Run (1)
episodes.summary: Michael accidentally runs over Meredith in the parking lot which results in Michael arranging a 5K fun run for her.

episodes.name: Fun Run (2)
episodes.summary: Michael accidentally runs over Meredith in the parking lot which results in Michael arranging a 5K fun run for her

episodes.name: Dunder Mifflin Infinity (1)
episodes.summary: Michael is resistant to the new ideas the Ryan brings to the company; Jim and Pam's relationship is exposed to the entire office.

episodes.name: Dunder Mifflin Infinity (2)
episodes.summary: Michael sets out to win clients back the old fashion way.

episodes.name: Launch Party (1)
episodes.summary: The Dunder Mifflin website launches; Dwight gets into a paper selling war with the new website.

episodes.name: Launch Party (2)
episodes.summary: Michael is upset when he realizes he wasn't invited to attend the actual launch party in New York; Andy pursues Angela.

episodes.name: Money (1)
episodes.summary: Michael takes a second job as supporting Jan is draining his finances; Jim and Pam stay at Dwight's farm.

episodes.name: Money (2)
episodes.summary: Oscar helps Michael with his finances; Andy continues to pursue Angela.

shows.name: BoJack Horseman
shows.summary: Meet the most beloved sitcom horse of the '90s, 20 years later. He's a curmudgeon with a heart of... not quite gold... but something like gold. Copper?

episodes.name: The BoJack Horseman Story, Chapter One
episodes.summary: To help with the memoir he hopes will put him back in the spotllight, BoJack hires a ghostwriter.

episodes.name: BoJack Hates the Troops
episodes.summary: BoJack finds himself the subject of national media attention after he calls the troops ''jerks''.

episodes.name: Prickly-Muffin
episodes.summary: BoJack reconnects with a friend from his past who moves into his house and creates pandemonium.

episodes.name: Zoës and Zeldas
episodes.summary: BoJack decides to mentor Todd; Diane's ex-boyfriend writes an article about Mr. Peanutbutter.

episodes.name: Live Fast, Diane Nguyen
episodes.summary: A business trip for BoJack and Diana takes a detour; Todd runs a scam that gets him in trouble.

episodes.name: Our A-Story Is a 'D' Story
episodes.summary: BoJack is jealous of Diane's relationship wtih Mr. Peanutbutter; Todd's in a new environment.

episodes.name: Say Anything
episodes.summary: BoJack sabotages himself with an epic bender; Princess Carolyn's agency merges with another.

episodes.name: The Telescope
episodes.summary: When he learns that his old friend from ''Horsin' Around'' is dying, BoJack tries to mend fences.

episodes.name: Horse Majeure
episodes.summary: A lovestruck BoJack tries to sabotage a wedding; Todd accepts a surprising new professional role.

episodes.name: One Trick Pony
episodes.summary: BoJack shoots a movie at Mr. Peanutbutter's house; Diane finishes her book.

episodes.name: Downer Ending
episodes.summary: BoJack embarks on a project in his typically gonzo style, leading to a drug-fueled revelation.

episodes.name: Later
episodes.summary: Months after his memoir is released, BoJack's being considered for a role that's a lifelong dream.

episodes.name: Brand New Couch
episodes.summary: BoJack believes an upbeat attitude will change his life, but that attitude doesn't mesh well with his new acting job.

episodes.name: Yesterdayland
episodes.summary: BoJack is drawn to the one female in town who has no idea who he is (because she was in a coma). Todd opens a theme park.

episodes.name: Still Broken
episodes.summary: A funeral turns into a treasure hunt for the ''Horsin' Around'' cast, and a potential schmooze-fest for Princess Carolyn and Mr. Peanutbutter.

episodes.name: After the Party
episodes.summary: Princess Carolyn, BoJack, Diane and Mr. Peanutbutter learn some important lessons about love in the wake of a surprise birthday party.

episodes.name: Chickens
episodes.summary: BoJack really wants his director Kelsey to like him. Todd, Diane and Kelsey's daughter help a chicken who's on the lam from the police.

episodes.name: Higher Love
episodes.summary: Princess Carolyn sees an opportunity when Mr. Peanutbutter's agent dies. BoJack flips when he says the ''L'' word out loud to Wanda.

episodes.name: Hank After Dark
episodes.summary: Diane goes off script during BoJack's book tour, jeopardizing Mr. Peanutbutter's new show. Todd gets involved in international affairs.

episodes.name: Let's Find Out
episodes.summary: BoJack is one of the celebrity guest stars on the first episode of Mr. Peanutbutter's game show, but the game doesn't go as expected.

episodes.name: The Shot
episodes.summary: BoJack cooks up a scheme with Kelsey, Todd, Princess Carolyn and Mr. Peanutbutter to steal a crucial shot for the Secretariat film.

episodes.name: Yes And
episodes.summary: Todd gets sucked into the cult of improv comedy. Wanda thinks Diane is a bad influence on BoJack when he has to deal with a career crisis.

episodes.name: Escape from L.A.
episodes.summary: BoJack goes on a road trip to visit an old friend, and gets a taste of existence outside the Hollywood bubble.

episodes.name: Out to Sea
episodes.summary: The important things in life become clear to BoJack when Todd moves out to join the improv comedy group on their special cruise ship.

episodes.name: Start Spreading the News
episodes.summary: Ana Spanakopita sends BoJack to New York to do interviews, and Todd tags along. Princess Carolyn tells Mr. Peanutbutter to get back to work.

episodes.name: The BoJack Horseman Show
episodes.summary: In 2007, Princess Carolyn finds a new project for BoJack, Diane meets Mr. Peanutbutter at the coffee shop where she works, and Todd kisses a girl.

episodes.name: BoJack Kills
episodes.summary: BoJack is framed for the murder of a Whale World dancer. Mr. Peanutbutter and Todd get into a stinky situation with the skunk next door.

episodes.name: Fish Out of Water
episodes.summary: BoJack takes an underwater trip to the Pacific Ocean Film Festival for the "Secretariat" premiere, where he tries to reach out to Kelsey.

episodes.name: Love and/or Marriage
episodes.summary: While Todd and BoJack crash a rehearsal dinner, Diane gets high with a client. Princess Carolyn goes on a series of blind dates.

episodes.name: Brrap Brrap Pew Pew
episodes.summary: Diane's tweet lands Sextina Aquafina in hot water. Prankster and A-list actor Jurj Clooners gets under BoJack's skin.

episodes.name: Stop the Presses
episodes.summary: Todd builds a giant papier-mâché Todd head. A customer service rep gives BoJack some sound advice when he tries to cancel his newspaper subscription.

episodes.name: Old Acquaintance
episodes.summary: Bojack is up for a part in David Pincher's latest film. Diane meets Mr. Peanutbutter's brother and Todd takes the business in a new direction.

episodes.name: Best Thing That Ever Happened
episodes.summary: BoJack meets Princess Carolyn at Elefante, leading to a night of soul-searching as they help the staff impress a food critic.

episodes.name: It's You
episodes.summary: Mr. Peanutbutter announces the Oscar nominees. BoJack surrounds himself with admirers, but his real friendships are falling apart.

episodes.name: That's Too Much, Man!
episodes.summary: On a drug-fueled bender, BoJack and Sarah Lynn crash an AA meeting, and BoJack decides to make amends to the people he hurt.

episodes.name: That Went Well
episodes.summary: Mr. Peanutbutter saves the day. Todd gets exciting news. BoJack, Diane and Princess Carolyn pursue new opportunities.

episodes.name: See Mr. Peanutbutter Run
episodes.summary: Mr. Peanutbutter's campaign to recall the governor of California culminates in a high-stakes ski race. Meanwhile, BoJack is nowhere to be found.

episodes.name: The Old Sugarman Place
episodes.summary: BoJack goes off the grid and winds up at his grandparents' dilapidated home in Michigan, where he befriends a dragonfly haunted by the past.

episodes.name: Hooray! Todd Episode!
episodes.summary: Helpful to a fault, Todd is spread thin doing favors for Princess Carolyn, Mr. Peanutbutter and a visitor with a potential connection to BoJack.

episodes.name: Commence Fracking
episodes.summary: The campaign takes a toll on Diane's love life. BoJack helps Hollyhock search for her mom. Princess Carolyn tries to get pregnant.

episodes.name: Thoughts and Prayers
episodes.summary: A mass shooting at a mall creates a PR nightmare for Princess Carolyn. BoJack takes Hollyhock to visit his estranged mother.

episodes.name: Stupid Piece of Sh*t
episodes.summary: Surrounded by family, BoJack spirals into self-loathing. Princess Carolyn and Rutabaga Rabitowitz plan Courtney and Todd's sham wedding.

episodes.name: Underground
episodes.summary: Mr. Peanutbutter's posh campaign fundraiser takes a terrifying turn. As chaos swirls around them, BoJack and Diane get drunk.

episodes.name: The Judge
episodes.summary: Hollyhock starts dating an intern on BoJack's new show. Princess Carolyn meets Ralph's parents. Mr. Peanutbutter throws his support behind Woodchuck.

episodes.name: Ruthie
episodes.summary: On one awful day, Princess Carolyn deals with rejection, deception and loss. BoJack and Diane try to track down Hollyhock's birth certificate.

episodes.name: Lovin' That Cali Lifestyle!!
episodes.summary: Diane leaks damaging information on Jessica Biel, Todd's clown dentist business hits a snag, and Princess Carolyn finds hope in an unlikely place.

episodes.name: Time's Arrow
episodes.summary: In 1963, young socialite Beatrice Sugarman meets the rebellious Butterscotch Horseman at her debutante party.

episodes.name: What Time Is It Right Now
episodes.summary: Princess Carolyn pitches "Philbert" to company execs. Todd gets a better business idea. BoJack comes to a realization about Hollyhock.

episodes.name: The Light Bulb Scene
episodes.summary: BoJack has misgivings about his new show. Princess Carolyn visits an adoption agency. Todd interviews for a job at WhatTimeIsItRightNow.com.

episodes.name: The Dog Days Are Over
episodes.summary: Diane divorces Mr. Peanutbutter and moves into a shabby studio. Feeling melancholy, she travels to Vietnam to get away from it all.

episodes.name: Planned Obsolescence
episodes.summary: Todd meets Yolanda's parents, who don't know she's asexual. Mr. Peanutbutter romances a young waitress. Gina confesses a childhood dream to BoJack.

episodes.name: BoJack the Feminist
episodes.summary: When Princess Carolyn casts a disgraced celeb in "Philbert", BoJack inadvertently takes a stand. Mr. Peanutbutter tries to toughen up his image.

episodes.name: The Amelia Earhart Story
episodes.summary: Painful memories come rushing back when Princess Carolyn visits her hometown and meets a pregnant teen. A stunt goes awry on the "Philbert" set.

episodes.name: Free Churro
episodes.summary: BoJack delivers a eulogy at a funeral.

episodes.name: INT. SUB
episodes.summary: Diane's therapist encourages her to set boundaries with BoJack. A missing string cheese ignites a dispute between Todd and Princess Carolyn.

episodes.name: Mr. Peanutbutter's Boos
episodes.summary: At BoJack's 25th annual Halloween party, Pickles gets a crash course in Mr. Peanutbutter's romantic history -- and some advice from Diane.

episodes.name: Ancient History
episodes.summary: A visiting Hollyhock dumps BoJack's painkillers, sending him on a desperate search for more. Todd tries to solve Emily's dating dilemma.

episodes.name: Head in the Clouds
episodes.summary: At the "Philbert" premiere party, Princess Carolyn deftly negotiates a deal, Gina steps into the limelight, and Diane confronts BoJack.

episodes.name: The Showstopper
episodes.summary: "Philbert" is a hit, and filming begins on Season 2. But as BoJack spirals deeper into addiction, he loses his grip on reality.

episodes.name: The Stopped Show
episodes.summary: In the midst of the latest PR crisis, Princess Carolyn gets a life-changing opportunity. With Diane's help, BoJack finally faces the music.

episodes.name: A Horse Walks into a Rehab
episodes.summary: BoJack checks into Pastiches, a Malibu rehab facility, where he grapples with memories of Sarah Lynn's death and his own troubled childhood.

episodes.name: The New Client
episodes.summary: When her nanny quits, Princess Carolyn struggles to balance the demands of work and parenting. Mr. Peanutbutter visits BoJack in rehab.

episodes.name: Feel-Good Story
episodes.summary: Girl Croosh sends Diane on the road with rugged cameraman Guy, but she balks when they ask her to produce more feel-good stories.

episodes.name: Surprise!
episodes.summary: Todd organizes a surprise wedding for Pickles and Mr. Peanutbutter, but the party quickly goes off the rails when Mr. Peanutbutter makes a confession.

episodes.name: A Little Uneven, Is All
episodes.summary: Dr. Champ tells BoJack it's time to leave rehab. Mr. Peanutbutter's reputation takes a hit. In Chicago, Diane wrestles with writer's block.

episodes.name: The Kidney Stays in the Picture
episodes.summary: The assistants of Hollywoo go on strike. BoJack tries to help Dr. Champ. When Todd learns that his mother needs a kidney, Diane comes up with a plan.

episodes.name: The Face of Depression
episodes.summary: BoJack travels around the country reconnecting with loved ones, while Mr. Peanutbutter embarks on his own national tour as the face of depression.

episodes.name: A Quick One, While He's Away
episodes.summary: A reporter digs into the circumstances surrounding Sarah Lynn's death. Hollyhock goes to a party in New York City.

episodes.name: Intermediate Scene Study w/ BoJack Horseman
episodes.summary: When BoJack starts teaching an acting class at Wesleyan, Hollyhock sets some boundaries in their relationship.

episodes.name: Good Damage
episodes.summary: Diane's depression lifts, but she's still struggling to start writing her memoir. Reporters Paige and Max pay Penny a visit.

episodes.name: Sunk Cost and All That
episodes.summary: While Paige and Max question Mr. Peanutbutter at his restaurant, a panicked BoJack tries to piece together what's happening.

episodes.name: Xerox of a Xerox
episodes.summary: After the Sarah Lynn story breaks, BoJack gives a live interview on TV. Diane meets Guy's teenage son.

episodes.name: The Horny Unicorn
episodes.summary: BoJack gets a new AA sponsor. Todd plans a sophisticated party to impress his parents. Diane gets to know Sonny.

episodes.name: Angela
episodes.summary: Angela Diaz reaches out to BoJack. Princess Carolyn gets an offer from Lenny Turtletaub. Todd cooks up a scheme.

episodes.name: The View from Halfway Down
episodes.summary: BoJack reconnects with faces from his past.

episodes.name: Nice While It Lasted
episodes.summary: A celebration brings people together.

shows.name: Orange Is the New Black
shows.summary: Orange Is the New Black is the story of Piper Chapman, a woman in her thirties who is sentenced to fifteen months in prison after being convicted of a decade-old crime of transporting money for her drug-dealing girlfriend.

episodes.name: I Wasn't Ready
episodes.summary: Sentenced to 15 months for a crime committed in her youth, Piper Chapman leaves her supportive fiancé Larry for her new home: a women's prison.

episodes.name: Tit Punch
episodes.summary: After insulting the food in front of the prison chef, Piper is starved out by the kitchen staff and struggles to offer up an acceptable apology.

episodes.name: Lesbian Request Denied
episodes.summary: Targeted for romance by a fellow prisoner, Piper finds that subtlety is an ineffective approach to letting her suitor down.

episodes.name: Imaginary Enemies
episodes.summary: Piper gets to know her stern new roommate; the prisoners prepare a farewell party for one of their own; a misplaced screwdriver has dire consequences.

episodes.name: The Chickening
episodes.summary: When her sighting of a legendary feral chicken polarizes the inmates, Larry wonders if Piper is getting too absorbed in the "fishbowl" of prison life.

episodes.name: WAC Pack
episodes.summary: The inmates campaign along racial lines for positions on a prisoners council, but Piper tries to stay above the increasingly raucous competition.

episodes.name: Blood Donut
episodes.summary: Piper wants the prison's outdoor running track reopened, but in order to get it, she'll have to give her corrections officer something he wants.

episodes.name: Moscow Mule
episodes.summary: Red gets pressured to smuggle drugs through her kitchen; Larry publishes an article about Piper in the New York Times; two babies come into the world.

episodes.name: Fucksgiving
episodes.summary: Thanksgiving arrives with the promise of a visit by Larry, but Piper's raunchy dance moves land her in solitary with a holiday feast of moldy bologna.

episodes.name: Bora Bora Bora
episodes.summary: Pennsatucky discovers a new skill; the inmates try to scare delinquent teenagers straight; Pornstache's side business suffers a tragic setback.

episodes.name: Tall Men with Feelings
episodes.summary: The prisoners mourn one of their own and even a drunken Pornstache reveals surprising emotions; Larry gives a revealing radio interview.

episodes.name: Fool Me Once
episodes.summary: Painful truths reorient several relationships and careers; Pennsatucky feels disrespected by Piper, which is not good; Larry delivers an ultimatum.

episodes.name: Can't Fix Crazy
episodes.summary: Red's scheme to reclaim her kitchen backfires; the inmates stage a Christmas pageant; Piper's plans unravel even as she realizes her life is in jeopardy.

episodes.name: Thirsty Bird
episodes.summary: Piper is forced to face the consequences of her actions in the Season 2 premiere.

episodes.name: Looks Blue, Tastes Red
episodes.summary: Taystee shows off her business skills. Meanwhile, Red feels isolated.

episodes.name: Hugs Can Be Deceiving
episodes.summary: Morello gets her heart broken. Later, someone from Taystee's past shows up.

episodes.name: A Whole Other Hole
episodes.summary: Sophia gives the women a lesson in female anatomy. Meanwhile, Larry makes changes to his life. Lorna takes a risk away from the prison when she finds out her Christopher is getting married.

episodes.name: Low Self Esteem City
episodes.summary: Gloria and Vee face off. Later, Piper receives devastating news about a family member.

episodes.name: You Also Have a Pizza
episodes.summary: The inmates prepare for a Valentine's Day party. Later, Red makes an interesting discovery.

episodes.name: Comic Sans
episodes.summary: Piper starts a prison newsletter. Meanwhile, Vee launches an entrepreneurial venture.

episodes.name: Appropriately Sized Pots
episodes.summary: Piper faces backlash over special privileges. Meanwhile, Caputo feels pressure to toughen up.

episodes.name: 40 Oz. of Furlough
episodes.summary: Piper's relationship with Larry is tested. Meanwhile, Red attempts to redeem herself.While Red receives recognition for her strives towards redemption, Piper's and Larry's relationship is tested. Meanwhile, Litchfield welcomes back a familiar face.

episodes.name: Little Mustachioed Shit
episodes.summary: The guards crack down on prison contraband. Later, a secret is revealed. The prison guards finally crack down on the inmates to stop the smuggling issue. After Bennett framed Pornstache, problems arise. And a big secret between Piper and Polly is revealed.

episodes.name: Take a Break from Your Values
episodes.summary: Piper is shocked at an unexpected change in her status; Soso's hunger strike attracts new support that takes on a religious fervour.

episodes.name: It Was the Change
episodes.summary: Tensions run high as a prison power outage forces several issues to come to light; Piper finds herself compromised and is forced to think on her feet.

episodes.name: We Have Manners. We're Polite.
episodes.summary: In the Season 2 finale, Several futures hang in the balance as the inmates face and confront their worst nightmares; Life will never be the same again.

episodes.name: Mother's Day
episodes.summary: Caputo's kinder, gentler new regime includes organizing a Mother's Day fair for the inmates that brings up a LOT of mixed feelings about family.

episodes.name: Bed Bugs and Beyond
episodes.summary: Tempers flare when bed bugs invade, as Alex cracks, Red lashes out at Piper, Aleida interferes with Daya and Bennett, and Caputo gets bad news.

episodes.name: Empathy is a Boner Killer
episodes.summary: Nicky's stash situation gets complicated. Alex and Crazy Eyes try a new drama class. Red assists Healy with a personal matter.

episodes.name: Finger in the Dyke
episodes.summary: Big Boo comes up with a scheme to make money. Daya, Taystee and Crazy Eyes confront reality. Caputo tries to make a good impression on some visitors.

episodes.name: Fake It Till You Fake It Some More
episodes.summary: Rumors fly among the inmates about a new, higher-paying job assignment, especially when they have to take a test for it.

episodes.name: Ching Chong Chang
episodes.summary: Regime change isn't going over well with the staff, but Red makes it work for her. Lorna finds a way to meet men. Chang shows her private side.

episodes.name: Tongue-Tied
episodes.summary: Norma's healing powers draw more believers. Piper creates a new business venture. Caputo breaks in the new hires.

episodes.name: Fear, and Other Smells
episodes.summary: Piper gets creative in order to grow her business. Crazy Eyes's sci-fi sex story for drama class is a hit. Alex doesn't trust new inmate Lolly.

episodes.name: Where My Dreidel At
episodes.summary: Too many inmates seem to be getting religion, so a rabbi comes to visit and Leanne tries to organize Norma's followers.

episodes.name: A Tittin' and a Hairin'
episodes.summary: Pennsatucky, Piper, Crazy Eyes and Lorna get closer with their new admirers. Tensions between Sophia and Gloria, and Alex and Lolly escalate.

episodes.name: We Can Be Heroes
episodes.summary: Caputo and Piper confront labor issues. A miracle occurs in Norma's group. Crazy Eyes's erotica winds up in the hands of the staff.

episodes.name: Don't Make Me Come Back There
episodes.summary: Daya goes into labor. Sophia suffers a hate crime. Stella helps Piper with a business snag. Taystee takes on a new role.

episodes.name: Trust No Bitch
episodes.summary: Miracles and conversions occur, but Piper and Caputo know that some situations can't be dealt with through spiritual means.

episodes.name: Work That Body for Me
episodes.summary: With a major security breach and a lot of new inmates, Caputo has to call in the big guns. Things get a little too real for Crazy Eyes and Lolly.

episodes.name: Power Suit
episodes.summary: the newcomers stir up ethnic and domestic problems, but Maria sees an opportunity. Judy's special treatment raises eyebrowes.

episodes.name: (Don't) Say Anything
episodes.summary: A new job puts Taystee close to Caputo. Lorna has to get creative in her marriage. Soso and Poussey deal with some awkward truths.

episodes.name: Doctor Psycho
episodes.summary: Nothing stays hidden for long when emotions run high, but Red, Healy and Caputo try to keep the peace. Piper has a business competitor.

episodes.name: We'll Always Have Baltimore
episodes.summary: Company policies lead to a shortage of critical supplies and an eventful trip to a prison convention. Piscatella starts a new anti-gang initiative.

episodes.name: Piece of Shit
episodes.summary: Piper's plan to edge out the competition could backfire badly. Cindy finds a way to make Taystee's job pay off. Luschek gets some interesting mail.

episodes.name: It Sounded Nicer in My Head
episodes.summary: Paranoia strikes deep for Lolly and Judy, aggravating an already tense situation. Red sticks to a Russian tradition for an important occasion.

episodes.name: Friends in Low Places
episodes.summary: A new work detail doesn't go over well with the inmates. Judy seeks help from Poussey. Maria finds a place she can conduct business.

episodes.name: Turn Table Turn
episodes.summary: Ramos and Flores figure out ways to rebel against authority. A news item has an unexpected effect. Red and Lorna face personal disappointment.

episodes.name: Bunny, Skull, Bunny, Skull
episodes.summary: The movie night selection becomes controversial. Aleida makes an adjustment. Piper worries the prison punishments are getting too medieval.

episodes.name: People Persons
episodes.summary: Caputo's leadership is challenged and the inmates are in for a long night of lockdown after workers make an unsettling discovery.

episodes.name: The Animals
episodes.summary: Alliances shift among the prison "families" as Piscatella and his guards crack down. Poussey, Judy and Alex prefer to look ahead to the future.

episodes.name: Toast Can't Never Be Bread Again
episodes.summary: Corporate bureaucracy and simmering anger work against Caputo's efforts to keep a sensitive situation under control.

episodes.name: Riot FOMO
episodes.summary: As the standoff at the prison spirals into a full-blown riot, enterprising inmates take advantage of the confusion. Taystee confronts Caputo.

episodes.name: Fuck, Marry, Frieda
episodes.summary: Maria convenes a special assembly in the chapel as the inmates plot their next move. Frieda makes use of the survival skills she learned as a kid.

episodes.name: Pissters!
episodes.summary: Linda begins to see the prison in a new light, while Judy grows desperate to escape. With darkness falling, the inmates compile a list of demands.

episodes.name: Litchfield's Got Talent
episodes.summary: Red and Blanca dig for dirt on Piscatella. Suzanne conducts a séance. The guards show off their talents for "Litchfield Idol" judges Leanne and Angie.

episodes.name: Sing It, White Effie
episodes.summary: When the inmates' antics make the morning news, Flaca and Maritza soak up the spotlight. Brandy and her crew auction off Judy to the highest bidder.

episodes.name: Flaming Hot Cheetos, Literally
episodes.summary: Boo defends Doggett, Janae consoles Soso, and Lorna comes on to Nicky. While Alex lays low in the yard, Piper decides to take a stand.

episodes.name: Full Bush, Half Snickers
episodes.summary: Black Cindy concocts a scheme to distract Suzanne when she spirals out of control. Taystee and Piper search for ways to honor Poussey's memory.

episodes.name: Tied to the Tracks
episodes.summary: While Taystee sits down with a negotiator, Red and Blanca put their own plan into action. Gloria counsels Daya and asks Caputo for a favor.

episodes.name: The Tightening
episodes.summary: Red senses trouble, but the others are convinced she's just paranoid. Taystee and Black Cindy enlist an unlikely ally, and two old friends clash.

episodes.name: The Reverse Midas Touch
episodes.summary: Taystee tries to keep the negotiations on track. Angie comes up with an idea for fixing Leanne's finger. Piscatella's past is revealed.

episodes.name: Breaking the Fiberboard Ceiling
episodes.summary: Red and the others weigh their options. Gloria wrestles with her conscience as she moves forward with a plan. Lorna takes over the pharmacy.

episodes.name: Tattoo You
episodes.summary: Boo dabbles in blackmail; Nicky promises to help Lorna, Doggett makes a discovery, and Piper comes to a realization about Alex.

episodes.name: Storm-y Weather
episodes.summary: As chaos descends on Litchfield three days into the riot, the inmates wonder what the future holds and seek solace in loved ones.

episodes.name: Who Knows Better Than I
episodes.summary: The COs at Litchfield's maximum security unit size up the new arrivals. Off her medication, Suzanne hallucinates.

episodes.name: Shitstorm Coming
episodes.summary: Ordered to hand out harsh sentences, the feds search for scapegoats. Languishing on paid suspension, Caputo tries to break out of a rut.

episodes.name: Look Out for Number One
episodes.summary: Linda makes a power play, Piper digs for information about Alex, and the women get their first taste of a decades-old feud between cellblocks.

episodes.name: I'm the Talking Ass
episodes.summary: Aleida struggles to find work on the outside. Backed into a corner, Nicky pleads her case to Red. Taystee reaches out to Caputo for help.

episodes.name: Mischief Mischief
episodes.summary: Pranksters wreak havoc on Halloween. Luschek earns points with the other guards. As Linda faces a crisis, Donuts and Pennsatucky have a change of plans.

episodes.name: State of the Uterus
episodes.summary: While Daddy deals with backlash, Badison plots revenge. Flaca finds a co-host for her radio show. Lorna discovers that pregnancy has its perks.

episodes.name: Changing Winds
episodes.summary: Badison hustles to get back in Carol's good graces. Blanca longs to get pregnant -- and Nicky has a plan. Caputo and Figueroa's relationship evolves.

episodes.name: Gordons
episodes.summary: Taystee gets fan mail -- and an assist from an old friend. Piper lobbies Luschek to bring back kickball. Aleida tries out a new sales tactic.

episodes.name: Break the String
episodes.summary: While Daddy buys time with Barb, Daya looks for new ways to bring in oxy. Black Cindy's guilt takes a toll on her body. Red acquires a powerful ally.

episodes.name: Chocolate Chip Nookie
episodes.summary: Aleida seizes a business opportunity. An anonymous tip sends Caputo on a stakeout. Luschek tries to help Gloria, who worries she's in danger.

episodes.name: Well This Took a Dark Turn
episodes.summary: While Red and Carol scheme against Frieda, Suzanne watches Frieda's back. Lorna swears her allegiance to Barb as Nicky works to thwart another plot.

episodes.name: Double Trouble
episodes.summary: Carol and Barb prepare for war. Linda auditions inmates for a prison PR video. Alex makes a deal with Badison to keep Piper out of trouble.

episodes.name: Be Free
episodes.summary: Piper gets surprising news and a boost from her friends. The jury returns with a verdict, and the big kickball game is on.

episodes.name: Beginning of the End
episodes.summary: Piper struggles to adapt to life after Litchfield. Alex promises to keep her nose clean. Daddy's infidelity angers Daya.

episodes.name: Just Desserts
episodes.summary: Piper earns her keep by babysitting. Aleida pressures Hopper to go for a promotion. Random drug searches put the heat on Alex. Daya gets an offer.

episodes.name: And Brown is the New Orange
episodes.summary: A new warden ushers in big changes. Blanca reunites with an old friend. Suzanne tries to mend Taystee and Black Cindy's relationship.

episodes.name: How to Do Life
episodes.summary: Piper goes to work for her dad. Vinnie visits Lorna with news. A desperate Taystee asks Daya for a favor. Caputo leads a restorative justice class.

episodes.name: Minority Deport
episodes.summary: Aleida tries to keep her teen daughter out of trouble. Piper and Cal shed their responsibilities for a day. Blanca navigates the legal system.

episodes.name: Trapped in an Elevator
episodes.summary: Taystee takes advantage of her new position. Linda teaches Tamika how to pivot. Black Cindy and Maria make amends to people they've hurt.

episodes.name: Me as Well
episodes.summary: Piper plays the field. Caputo tries to get ahead of a scandal. Daya sends Taystee on a mission. Nicky finds romance.

episodes.name: Baker's Dozen
episodes.summary: Neri takes Piper on a wilderness retreat. Taystee tutors Pennsatucky. Suzanne tends to the chicken coop. Nicky has a difficult conversation with Red.

episodes.name: The Hidey Hole
episodes.summary: Competition forces Daya to find a new mule. Piper decides to come out as a felon. Karla asks to leave for a custody hearing. Shani confides in Nicky.

episodes.name: The Thirteenth
episodes.summary: While Piper reconnects with faces from her past, Alex revisits her own romantic history. Linda puts Tamika on notice. Caputo owns up to his misdeeds.

episodes.name: God Bless America
episodes.summary: Maria tries to make peace with Gloria. Karla and Blanca plead their cases. Figueroa stands up to Litvack. Piper attends a posh fundraiser with Zelda.

episodes.name: The Big House
episodes.summary: Taystee meets with a lawyer. As the GED exam looms, Suzanne gives Pennsatucky a pep talk. Gloria faces a dilemma. Alex confronts Piper.

episodes.name: Here's Where We Get Off
episodes.summary: Tearful farewells, emotional tributes, new beginnings. Say goodbye to the women of Litchfield in the series finale.

shows.name: Everybody Hates Chris
shows.summary: Inspired by his childhood experiences, comedian Chris Rock narrates the hilarious, touching story of a teenager growing up as the eldest of children in Brooklyn, New York during the early 1980s.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Pilot
episodes.summary: Uprooted to a new neighborhood and bussed to a predominantly white middle school two-hours away by his strict, hard-working parents, Chris struggles to find his place while keeping his siblings in line at home and dealing with the school bully, Joey.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Keisha
episodes.summary: When Chris's mother, Rochelle, hires the girl next door, Keisha, to tutor Drew at Math, Chris sees this as an ideal chance to impress her. But someone else may have the same intentions. Meanwhile, Julius finds himself in an awkward position when he becomes attracted to another woman, Sheila.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Basketball
episodes.summary: Chris joins the school Basketball team even though he can't play, but a failed pop quiz threatens to get him dropped. Back at home Julius gets a lodger after he offers 6 months rent up front, despite the fact he makes Rochelle nervous.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Sausage
episodes.summary: Fists fly and Dr. Raymond (Jude Ciccolella) must intervene in the conflict between Chris and the school bully.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Fat Mike
episodes.summary: Despite his parents' warnings, Chris lends his bike to a kid who disappears with it; While Julius is on strike, he proves to be a better housekeeper than Rochelle which makes her very angry.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Halloween
episodes.summary: In order to impress a girl Chris goes to an adult party where the girl will be. With some urging from his friend he decides to go as the eccentric singer Prince.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Babysitter
episodes.summary: When Rochelle and Julius go out for the night, they hire a sitter to look after Chris, Drew, and Tonya. But the babysitter keeps leaving the house and Chris covers for her. Julius and Rochelle's night-out is anything but romantic when the restaurant gets robbed.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Laundromat
episodes.summary: Chris spends all day at the laundromat while watching his younger brother and sister. His parents try to get a good deal on a new TV.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Food Stamps
episodes.summary: Julius finds $200 worth of food stamps but Rochelle is too embarrassed to use them; Chris and Greg have a big argument over their science project.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Greg
episodes.summary: When Chris decides to hang out and play games at Greg's house without parental permission, Greg's father comes home, so Chris must hide to avoid getting caught.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Christmas
episodes.summary: Rochelle and Julius don't have enough money to get Chris his new Walkman that he wants for Christmas. Drew tells Toyna the truth about Santa Claus, which makes Tonya question about other things her mom and dad told her.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates a Part-Time Job
episodes.summary: After deciding to buy a leather jacket, Chris goes to work with his dad to earn money and quickly learns the value of a dollar.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Picture Day
episodes.summary: Chris gets a new outfit for school picture day, but Caruso ends up stealing it. Drew hides Toyna's shoe when she breaks his toy. Rochelle sells make-up at home to make some extra money.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Valentine's Day
episodes.summary: Chris gets a bunch of "I hate you" cards from Caruso and also develops a crush on a girl named Tangee on his bus ride. Tonya reveals that she has a boyfriend which surprises Julius, and Drew gets a bunch of Valentines' cards from a lot of girls.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Lottery
episodes.summary: Julius and Rochelle struggle to give up the things they like: chocolate turtles and lottery tickets. Meanwhile, Chris claims to be a pro at "Asteroids", but when he hears that someone beat his score, it's up to him to defend his title.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Gout
episodes.summary: Chris gets an "F" on his report card and tries to figure out a way to change his grade before his parents find out. When Julius is sick with the gout, he starts watching The Young and the Restless.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Funerals
episodes.summary: When Rochelle's dad, Gene unexpectedly dies during a family dinner, Rochelle starts acting less than normal and Chris is shocked to see his mother not acting like herself.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Corleone
episodes.summary: Sick of the constant bullying from Caruso, Chris attempts to get kicked out of Corleone. Meanwhile, Julius' smelly new job may drive the family away.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Drew
episodes.summary: Fed up with Drew being the best at everything, Chris decides to take up karate. But when the two brothers challenge each other, Chris ends up breaking Drew's hand. Meanwhile, Toyna gets a lesson in gossip at the beauty salon.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Playboy
episodes.summary: Chris finds a Playboy magazine in Julius' toolbox and takes it to school, but the assistant principal confiscates it, Chris attempts to get it back. Julius is surprised when he finds out that Rochelle has money stashed in their room. Toyna teases Drew because of his fear of spiders.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Jail
episodes.summary: Chris has to sell cookies in order to go on a field trip to Washington D.C. Meanwhile Julius and Rochelle had tickets for Dreamgirls but Drew and Tonya get sick.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Father's Day
episodes.summary: Chris tries to find a gift for Julius for Father's Day, but all Julius wants is a day to himself.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Rejection
episodes.summary: Chris asks a girl on a date, but gets stood up; Rochelle clashes with new neighbor Louise over the establishment of a neighborhood watch program.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Class President
episodes.summary: Chris runs for 8th grade class president, and Julius is diagnosed with high blood pressure.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Elections
episodes.summary: Chris has to prepare a speech as part of his campaign for class president; Mr. Omar the funeral director moves into the rental apartment but winds up increasing Julius's stress.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates a Liar
episodes.summary: Chris leads the neighborhood into thinking that he and Tasha are a couple. Drew feels left out when Rochelle doesn't have enough time for him. Julius is obsessed with trading stamps.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Malvo
episodes.summary: When Chris is robbed at work by the neighborhood's well-known criminal Malvo, he is scared to tell Doc who robbed the place. Julius gets a Betamax and is upset when Tonya accidentally tapes over Julius' stories.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Buddy System
episodes.summary: The new vice-principal pairs Chris and Caruso up as buddies on a school field trip, but Chris and Caruso end up being left behind. Toyna takes Rochelle's hoop earrings and then loses one of them. Julius searches for a Wayne Gretzky hockey jersey for Drew.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Promises
episodes.summary: Chris finds out that he can't fulfill his campaign promises as class president, causing him to become increasingly unpopular at school; Rochelle's brother comes to stay with the family temporarily but shows little interest in finding a job or moving out.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Thanksgiving
episodes.summary: Julius' successful younger brother joins Chris' family for Thanksgiving dinner and Julius works hard to prepare the perfect Thanksgiving meal.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Superstition
episodes.summary: Chris's luck changes after he borrows a pair of socks from his father; Drew and Tonya conspire to get their parents to buy them things.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Kris
episodes.summary: Julius works at a department store dressed up as Santa Claus along with Chris dressed up as an elf, but Julius gets fired for freaking the kids out and Chris ends up in the hospital.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Eggs
episodes.summary: For a class project, Chris has to take care of an egg as if it were a baby; Julius looks for ways to save money on the electric bill.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Hall Monitors
episodes.summary: Chris' classmates do not take him seriously when he signs up to be a hall monitor; Tonya discovers that her friends hang out with her to get closer to Drew.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Snow Day
episodes.summary: On a snowy day, Chris arrives at school only to find that classes have been canceled and he is expected to spend the day with Principal Edwards, the only other person there.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Substitute
episodes.summary: A black substitute teacher takes over Chris' class while Ms. Morello is away, but the teacher ends up giving Chris a hard time, prompting Chris to figure out a way to get rid of him. Toyna discovers that she can her own way by accusing Drew of hitting her, and Mr. Omar gets on Julius' last nerve.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Cutting School
episodes.summary: With their teacher Ms. Morello out for the day, Chris and Greg decide to cut school and go see the movie "Ghostbusters".

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Chain Snatching
episodes.summary: When Chris stops Malvo trying to steal a gold chain from Vanessa, Malvo demands that Chris get him a gold chain. Tonya tries to win tickets to a Billy Ocean concert. Rochelle discovers that Julius has been hiding a credit card from her.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates DJs
episodes.summary: Chris decides to become a DJ, but his new career takes a wrong turn when he accidentally scratches Rochelle's James Brown album. Drew asks Julius to take him to a magic show, but Julius is afraid of rabbits.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Baseball
episodes.summary: Chris is stuck between a rock and a hard place when he has to make a decision about going to a baseball game with Julius and Drew or go to the movies with Tasha. Rochelle forgets to mail Tonya's letter to the Billy Ocean fan club. Rochelle's brother, Michael asks Vanessa out on a date.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Gambling
episodes.summary: Chris becomes Bed-Stuy's favorite odds-maker after Doc discovers he has a natural talent for predicting a game's outcome.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Dirty Jokes
episodes.summary: Chris starts telling dirty jokes after listening to his parents' comedy records. Rochelle tries to find a man for her mother, Maxine. Drew avoids a girl in a neighborhood who likes him.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Math
episodes.summary: Chris needs to get a good grade in algebra so the class will get a pizza party. Meanwhile, Drew breaks his dad's fav chair.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Last Day
episodes.summary: Chris is trying to get back at the school bully. Meanwhile, Drew wants to wear his graduation grown and cap, but something happens to it.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Guidance Counselor
episodes.summary: At the end of the 8th grade, Mrs. Morello hands out the Grade Eight Proficiency Assessment Test (GEPA) and Chris doesn't study so he thinks it doesn't count. So, he purposely does bad on the test, which makes him See Mr. Avich, the guidance counselor, who tries to get Chris into a special needs class.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Caruso
episodes.summary: Caruso is beat up by one of Chris's classmates. As a result, the absence of Caruso's power throws the entire school into chaos.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Driving
episodes.summary: Julius allows Chris to park the family car across the street, but he takes the car to school instead. Meanwhile, Rochelle goes to court to dispute a speeding ticket.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Blackie
episodes.summary: Chris is allowed to get a dog after the house is robbed. The dog turns out to be impossible to train, until Chris discovers that the dog only understands Spanish.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Bachelor Pad
episodes.summary: Since the rest of the family is sick, Chris is forced to spend a few nights at Mr. Omar's apartment, where he discovers Richard Pryor for the first time.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Bed-Stuy
episodes.summary: After Chris's first article for the school paper is rejected, he creates a bogus story about a serial killer in Bed-Stuy--which only gets him in more trouble.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Houseguests
episodes.summary: Greg's friendship with Chris is put to the test when he spends the week at Chris's house, and Julius has an eventful first week as a taxicab driver.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Minimum Wage
episodes.summary: When Doc refuses to give Chris a raise, Chris goes out and gets a new job at the local Chinese restaurant.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the New Kid
episodes.summary: When a second black kid enrolls in Chris's school, the two become fast friends, which makes Greg jealous. Julius becomes nervous when the IRS gives the family a larger-than-expected tax refund.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Kwanzaa
episodes.summary: Julius talks the family into celebrating Kwanza instead of Christmas this year because Kwanza is cheaper. Meanwhile, Chris helps Kill Moves find his mother as part of his school Christmas assignment.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Port Authority
episodes.summary: The family plans to go to a relative's funeral in South Carolina, but Chris and Julius lose the ticket money to a three-card monte dealer.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Bad Boys
episodes.summary: Chris models himself after a popular rapper and takes on a bad boy persona in order to impress Tasha. Julius gets the family a free dinner at an expensive restaurant, but the restaurant is less than hospitable.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the First Kiss
episodes.summary: Chris tries to get himself invited to a spin-the-bottle party so that he can get a chance to kiss Tasha. He winds up kissing another girl, which only causes unforeseen problems.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Easter
episodes.summary: Chris plans to take Tasha to an Easter pageant after she breaks up with her boyfriend; Rochelle seeks to win the hat competition at church.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Gretzky
episodes.summary: Chris accompanies Drew as they skip school to get Wayne Gretzky's autograph for Drew.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the BFD
episodes.summary: Chris accepts a $250 Black Funeral Directors scholarship from Mr. Omar so that he can pay for Run-DMC concert tickets, but he gets more than he bargained for.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Ex-Cons
episodes.summary: Malvo is out of prison. But instead of coming after Chris, he asks for his help in getting him into high school. Julius and Drew put a wager on a Mets/Dodgers series, and Tanya's new friend uses her as an alibi to lie to her mom.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Earth Day
episodes.summary: Chris tries to enlist Julius to help him collect cans for an Earth Day project. Meanwhile, Tonya is reprimanded at school while mimicking her mother, causing Rochelle to try and stop yelling anymore.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Being Cool
episodes.summary: Chris and Greg try to become cool, and Chris gets suspended from school when Greg is busted with a cigarette and blames it on Chris. And that's where the real trouble begins for him.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Ninth-Grade Dance
episodes.summary: Chris catches a lot of grief for asking a white girl from his class to the ninth grade dance, so he backs out of it, which only makes things worse. Julius and Drew help out a relative starting a business, and Tanya takes up ballet.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Mother's Day
episodes.summary: Chris buys a knock-off version of an expensive perfume from Risky as a Mother's Day present, and Rochelle gets a rash from it. Julius discovers that he talked Drew into giving away an old toy that is worth $150.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Graduation
episodes.summary: Chris tries to get accepted into the Bronx Science Academy so that he can go to high school with Greg, Julius helps his brother start a Mix Tape business not knowing that they're illegal, and Drew helps Tanya with her ballet recital.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Tattaglia
episodes.summary: Chris is relieved to find out that he is not the only black person at his new high school. However, he does run into problems because he is the only non-white student in his homeroom class, and the teacher can't stand him.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Cake
episodes.summary: An androgynous boy in Chris' home ec class helps him meet a cute classmate. Rochelle learns that Tasha's mom is an ex-con. Drew and Tonya get a job babysitting Mr. Omar's fish.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Homecoming
episodes.summary: Chris gets a cute girl to go to the homecoming dance with him, and discovers that her parents are the Huxtables. But he may be forced to ditch her when he finds out that Greg is down and needs his attention.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the English Teacher
episodes.summary: Chris talks the English teacher to show movies based on books instead of reading the actual books, but their friendship is short-lived. Julius' plan to raise Mr. Omar's rent backfires when his apartment fails inspection.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates My Man
episodes.summary: Chris gets in over his head when he helps a star football player with his studies. Julius suddenly has a new outlook on life when he takes a job helping Mr. Omar at the funeral home.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Doc's
episodes.summary: Chris can't stand working for Doc's new young girlfriend at the store, so he deliberately breaks them up. Meanwhile, Tanya starts wearing a stuffed bra to school.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Snitches
episodes.summary: Chris' plan to snitch on a shooter and collect the reward money backfires when Jerome is put in jail. Meanwhile, Julius sets up the perfect anniversary for Rochelle, only to see it accidentally fall apart.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Big Bird
episodes.summary: Chris starts dating one of the nerdiest girls in the school, but doesn't want anyone to know about it. Meanwhile, the entire neighborhood bets on whether the Giants or the Jets will lose first.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates James
episodes.summary: Ms. Morello forces Chris to become a "big brother," the family gets an answering machine, and Julius tries to get marital advice from a new talk show called "Oprah".

episodes.name: Everybody Hates New Year's Eve
episodes.summary: While Chris tries to find a way to get to Times Square for New Year's Eve, Julius becomes a hero after he talks a jumper down from the George Washington Bridge.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Mr. Levine
episodes.summary: Chris becomes friends with Mr. Levine, Rochelle finds out she's a year younger, and Greg tries to stop being called Chris and Greg.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Varsity Jackets
episodes.summary: Chris joins the wrestling team to get a varsity letter, Rochelle puts the entire family on a diet, and Drew tries to put together a girl group.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Fake IDs
episodes.summary: Chris and Greg try to get fake IDs so that they can go to a Fat Boys concert, Drew buys a pair of X-ray glasses, and Rochelle gets upset when Julius won't let her give him a surprise birthday party.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates PSATs
episodes.summary: Rochelle wants Chris to do well on the PSATs so that she can show him off to an elite African American club she wants the family to join. As a result, he cheats and gets a perfect score.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Boxing
episodes.summary: Mr. Thurman convinces Chris to take up boxing, a new neighbor has the hots for Julius, and Rochelle gets lonely for Julius while he takes on extra shifts.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Lasagna
episodes.summary: Chris is forced to hide some marijuana for a neighborhood punk on the run from the police. Out of fear, he decides to bake it in his lasagna for his cooking class.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Spring Break
episodes.summary: Greg breaks his leg after he is hit by a car, but Chris doesn't want anyone to know that he was driving the car. Meanwhile, Julius tries to take Tanya and Drew to Amityville for a beach vacation during Spring Break.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the Car
episodes.summary: Chris buys his first car, which only leads to a series of unforeseen problems. Meanwhile, everybody else in the house accidentally stumbles into everyone else's private business.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Back Talk
episodes.summary: Chris comes to blows with Rochelle when he refuses to clean the dishes, and Mr. Omar gives his notice to vacate because he believes he is dying.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Tasha
episodes.summary: Chris runs into unexpected problems when Tasha becomes his girlfriend, Rochelle finds out that Julius is still technically married to another woman, and Drew and Tonya have a contest to see who can be quiet the longest.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates Bomb Threats
episodes.summary: Chris calls in a bomb threat to get out of doing a punishwork assignment, and Rochelle tries to get rid of a "mojo" placed on her by one of her customers at the salon.

episodes.name: Everybody Hates the G.E.D.
episodes.summary: Chris opts to take the G.E.D. over repeating the 10th grade, Drew tries to go on Amateur Night at the Apollo, Julius tries to get $25 back from somebody who owes him, and Tanya graduates from sixth grade.

shows.name: Seinfeld
shows.summary: Seinfeld is the story of a fictionalized version of stand-up comedian Jerry Seinfeld, his life in New York City and his quirky group of friends who join him in wrestling with life's most perplexing, yet often trivial questions. Often described as "a show about nothing," Seinfeld mines the humor in life's mundane situations like waiting in line, searching for a lost item, or the trials and tribulations of dating.

episodes.name: Good News, Bad News
episodes.summary: Jerry is excited that a woman he met on the road is coming to visit him in New York. However, he has a tough time discerning whether her intentions are romantic or platonic.

episodes.name: The Stakeout
episodes.summary: When Jerry goes to a birthday party with Elaine, he meets a woman he'd like to ask out, but doesn't get the chance to. When she leaves before he gets a chance to find out her name, he and George stakeout her office building.

episodes.name: The Robbery
episodes.summary: When Jerry's apartment gets robbed, he decides to move out. George shows him a great apartment, which he plans to take… that is, until George expresses his own desire for the apartment.

episodes.name: Male Unbonding
episodes.summary: Jerry ponders a few courses of action when he sees that he no longer has anything in common with a clingy and obnoxious childhood friend. George has trouble with his latest relationship. Kramer has the idea to open a chain of pizza parlors where you make your own pizza pie.

episodes.name: The Stock Tip
episodes.summary: Jerry and George invest based on an insider stock tip, but then lose touch with their inside source. A nervous Jerry lets the declining stock ruin his weekend away with Vanessa, while George decides he's all in.

episodes.name: The Ex-Girlfriend
episodes.summary: Jerry begins to date George's ex - girlfriend before he discovers how clingy she is. Meanwhile George refuses to pay a physical therapist, convinced he did nothing and Elaine has a run - in with a friend who has stopped saying "hello" to her.

episodes.name: The Pony Remark
episodes.summary: Jerry worries that he may have killed an old relative, after he offends her with an off-the-cuff comment about hating kids who had ponies growing up.

episodes.name: The Jacket
episodes.summary: Jerry splurges on an expensive new suede jacket, but the colorful inner lining leaves something to be desired. When Elaine does Kramer a favor, Jerry and George get stuck alone with Elaine's intimidating, curmudgeonly father.

episodes.name: The Phone Message
episodes.summary: George regrets not going upstairs for "coffee" after a date, and proceeds to leave his girlfriend a series of increasingly embarrassing phone messages. Meanwhile, Jerry and his girlfriend argue over the comedic value of a TV commercial.

episodes.name: The Apartment
episodes.summary: Jerry helps Elaine get the apartment above his, but later regrets doing so. George's experiment of wearing a wedding band to attract women goes horribly awry.

episodes.name: The Statue
episodes.summary: Jerry finds a statue in a box of his grandfather's old things, that George wants to replace a similar one he broke as a child. When Jerry hires the boyfriend of an author Elaine is working with, to clean his apartment, the cleaning job is spectacular, but the statue goes missing.

episodes.name: The Revenge
episodes.summary: George quits his job in a huff, but comes to regret the decision, and is later humiliated by his boss. Jerry believes that the man at the laundromat has stolen a large sum of money from his laundry bag. Employing Elaine's and Kramer's help, respectively, they each plan revenge.

episodes.name: The Heart Attack
episodes.summary: George thinks he's had a heart attack. The doctor tells him otherwise, but he might want to get his tonsils and adenoids removed. Elaine is interested in the doctor and he is interested in her tongue. George not wanting to deal with the cost and the procedure decides to take Kramer's advice of going to see a naturalist, which only makes the situation worse.

episodes.name: The Deal
episodes.summary: Jerry and Elaine establish a new set of ground rules so they can remain friends but still sleep with each other often. With Jerry in a dilemma to find Elaine an "appropriate" birthday gift and with George's skepticism, their new deal is doomed from the start.

episodes.name: The Baby Shower
episodes.summary: When Jerry goes out of town, Elaine uses his apartment for the baby shower of a woman George once dated. However, the party goes awry when Kramer shows up to install illegal cable, Jerry's trip is cancelled, and George intends to confront the mother-to-be for mistreating him on their date years earlier.

episodes.name: The Chinese Restaurant
episodes.summary: Jerry, Elaine, and George endure a series of misadventures while waiting the entire episode to get a table at a Chinese restaurant.

episodes.name: The Busboy
episodes.summary: George continuously yet inadvertently meddles in the life of a busboy. Elaine has a man come to visit her for a week, we she soon finds is far too long for her liking.

episodes.name: The Note
episodes.summary: Jerry and George's massages end up being more pain than pleasure when Jerry has a misunderstanding with the masseusse and George gets his massage from a man. Jerry and George get Jerry's dentist friend into trouble when they ask him for doctor's notes for physical therapy. Meanwhile, Kramer is convinced he's seen Joe DiMaggio in a neighbourhood donut shop.

episodes.name: The Truth
episodes.summary: Jerry's audit becomes more trouble than he can handle when George breaks up with the former IRS worker who was helping Jerry, and she ends up in a sanitorium. Meanwhile, Kramer and Elaine have trouble getting along while Kramer dates Elaine's roommate.

episodes.name: The Pen
episodes.summary: Jerry and Elaine travel to Florida to see Jerry's parents which they soon regret when Elaine injures her back on a fold - out couch and Jerry gets caught up in a scandal by just saying that he liked someone's pen.

episodes.name: The Dog
episodes.summary: When a fellow airline passenger is sent to hospital, Jerry gets stuck watching his unruly dog for several days. With Jerry stuck in his apartment, George and Elaine discover they have very little to talk about on their own. Meanwhile, Kramer has difficulty breaking up with his girlfriend.

episodes.name: The Library
episodes.summary: Jerry is investigated by the library for a book he checked out in 1971 and never returned. Elaine fears for her career when her boss hates her recommendations, and Kramer strikes up a relationship with the librarian. Meanwhile, George believes that a homeless man is his old gym teacher, whom he got fired.

episodes.name: The Parking Garage
episodes.summary: The gang experience a series of misadventures while trying to find Kramer's car in a mall parking garage.

episodes.name: The Cafe
episodes.summary: Jerry tries helping the owner of a small restaurant attract customers by giving him a suggestion. George must take an IQ test for his latest girlfriend, so Elaine helps him in "a caper" where she takes the test for him. Kramer tries to keep his mother's ex-boyfriend's jacket, because it helps him meet women; however the ex-boyfriend comes looking for it.

episodes.name: The Tape
episodes.summary: While listening to a tape of his previous nights show, Jerry hears the voice of a mysterious woman who talks dirty into his tape recorder and everyone becomes excited by the voice. George tries a new cure for baldness. Kramer searches for the jacket.

episodes.name: The Nose Job
episodes.summary: Jerry's brain and penis play chess against one another to decide whether he should keep dating a vacuous model with whom the "sex is great" but not anything else. George is dating a woman with a big nose, Kramer tells her it like it is and she gets a nose job; however, there is a complication. Kramer gets Elaine to help him retrieve the jacket.

episodes.name: The Stranded
episodes.summary: Jerry & Elaine go to a party with George, while there they send signals to each other to get out of bad party conversations. Afterward, George abandons them there, when he leaves with a co-worker he has a chance with. Jerry & Elaine keeping the hosts up real late, while waiting for Kramer to come and pick them up. The host later takes Jerry up on his offer to drop by when he is in the city, just as Jerry is leaving. He stays in Jerry's apartment for the evening and parties with Kramer and a lady of the evening. Later George pays the price for his romance in the workplace (he must quit) and he tries to shoplift at the drug store where he says they still owe him ten dollars.

episodes.name: The Alternate Side
episodes.summary: Jerry's car is stolen and the criminal picks up his car phone. George decides to take a fill-in job moving cars from one side of the street to the other; this turns into a disaster for local traffic flow. Kramer gets a chance to do a line in a Woody Allen film; "these pretzels are making me thirsty." Elaine gets tired of her 66-year-old boyfriend and when she is about to break up with him, he has a stroke.

episodes.name: The Red Dot
episodes.summary: Jerry is the unwitting cause of Elaine's boyfriend falling off the wagon. Elaine gets George a job, so he buys her a gift, an $85 cashmere sweater. The sweater has a little red dot on it; hence the price. Elaine gives it back, then George gives it to the cleaning woman, to keep her quiet about them having sex on his desk. George gets fired.

episodes.name: The Subway
episodes.summary: Everyone has an uncommon experience while going their separate ways on the subway. George meets a beautiful woman who distracts him from his intended destination, a job interview. Jerry falls asleep and then wakes up across from a fat naked man. Elaine's train stops in the middle of a tunnel on her way to be best man at a lesbian wedding. Kramer overhears a hot tip on a horse on his way to pay $600 in traffic violations.

episodes.name: The Pez Dispenser
episodes.summary: Kramer joins the "Polar Bear Club." Jerry gets one of Kramer's Pez dispensers which makes Elaine laugh during a piano recital of George's girlfriend, and that puts their relationship in jeopardy. Kramer has an idea for a cologne that smells like the beach and suggests that George make a preemptive breakup with his girlfriend, that will give him the "upper hand." The Pez dispenser has a remarkable effect during a drug confrontation.

episodes.name: The Suicide
episodes.summary: Elaine needs to fast before an x-ray, so she tries stuffing herself three days before the test. After his neighbor Martin tries suicide, Jerry is hit on by his girlfriend, Gina, while at the hospital. A psychic warns George to cancel his vacation to the Cayman Islands, but never can tell George why. Jerry becomes worried when Newman (a friend of Martins) sees him with Gina. Elaine starts hallucinating from hunger. Everything hinges on a Drake's coffee cake.

episodes.name: The Fix-Up
